claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine whether you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children. Let me start with a few key questions.

**First, how many children are you inquiring about, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

I have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'm glad to help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

To determine your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about each child and your circumstances. Let me start with the basics.

**What are the dates of birth for Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 7 years old, Blake is 20, and Charlie is also 7. Alex and Charlie are twins.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So Alex and Charlie are 7-year-old twins, and Blake is 20 years old.

Before I proceed with more detailed questions, let me first read the eligibility criteria to ensure I'm asking the right questions.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, I've reviewed the eligibility rules. Now I have a key question based on Blake's age.

**Since Blake is 20 years old, are they currently in approved education or training (such as university, college, or an apprenticeship)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training. They actually work full-time now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. So Blake is working full-time and not in education or training.

Now I need to gather some information about your living situation and circumstances. Let me ask:

**Do you live with Alex, Blake, and Charlie in the same household, or do any of them live elsewhere?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, all three of them live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good to know, thank you. All three children live with you.

Now I need to ask about your own circumstances to determine eligibility. This is important for assessing your overall eligibility.

**What is your current employment status - are you employed, self-employed, unemployed, or in some other situation?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have one more important question about your residency status, as this affects eligibility.

**Are you a UK citizen, or do you have a specific immigration status such as settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, pre-settled status, or another type of visa?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for that information. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for all three children.

Based on the eligibility rules I've reviewed:

**For Alex (7 years old):**
- You live with Alex
- Alex is under 16
- You are responsible for Alex
- Alex meets all the basic eligibility criteria

**For Charlie (7 years old):**
- You live with Charlie
- Charlie is under 16
- You are responsible for Charlie
- Charlie meets all the basic eligibility criteria

**For Blake (20 years old):**
- Blake is 20 years old and no longer in approved education or training
- According to the rules, you normally only qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training
- Since Blake is 20 and working full-time (not in approved education or training), they no longer qualify

Let me now provide your formal eligibility outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary

Based on my assessment of your eligibility for Child Benefit:

**You are eligible for Child Benefit for:**
- ✅ **Alex** (age 7)
- ✅ **Charlie** (age 7)

**You are not eligible for Child Benefit for:**
- ❌ **Blake** (age 20)

**Why Blake doesn't qualify:** Since Blake is 20 years old and working full-time without being in approved education or training, they are no longer eligible. Child Benefit for children over 16 only continues if they remain in approved education or training and are under 20.

You can proceed to claim Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie. If you have any questions or your circumstances change, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for further guidance.

reasonings: Alex is 7 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant, and the claimant is responsible for their care. All basic eligibility criteria are met. Alex qualifies for Child Benefit.
Charlie is 7 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant, and the claimant is responsible for their care. All basic eligibility criteria are met. Charlie qualifies for Child Benefit.

Blake is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training - they are employed full-time. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit normally continues only if a child is under 20 and in approved education or training. Blake no longer meets this criterion and is therefore not eligible for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen employed and responsible for three children living in the same household. Alex and Charlie, both 7-year-old twins, are eligible for Child Benefit as they are under 16 and the claimant is responsible for their care. Blake, at 20 years old and working full-time without being in approved education or training, is no longer eligible for Child Benefit. The claimant can claim Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie only.

Charlie is 7 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant, and the claimant is responsible for their care. All basic eligibility criteria are met. Charlie qualifies for Child Benefit.

Blake is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training - they are employed full-time. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit normally continues only if a child is under 20 and in approved education or training. Blake no longer meets this criterion and is therefore not eligible for Child Benefit.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen employed and responsible for three children living in the same household. Alex and Charlie, both 7-year-old twins, are eligible for Child Benefit as they are under 16 and the claimant is responsible for their care. Blake, at 20 years old and working full-time without being in approved education or training, is no longer eligible for Child Benefit. The claimant can claim Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie only.
